title: lippia nodiflora var. lanceolata
intro: Lippia nodiflora var. lanceolata, commonly known as Mexican oregano, is a perennial shrub native to Mexico and Central America. It belongs to the Verbenaceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, antifungal, and antibacterial properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, such as colds, coughs, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Mexican oregano is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits, including immune system support and digestive relief.
